assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | WARN | Repetitive Test Detection: N/A (no test files), Test Documentation: N/A (no test files), Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate (11/13); overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56).' The Test Quality verdict is WARN, driven by the Eval Quality component being WARN due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 29-35 document the 3-criteria heuristic detection: Author 'github-actions[bot]' matches, body contains '## Eval Results' marker, body contains 'sdlc-workflow/run-evals' footer. Lines 39-53 show extracted eval metrics: per-eval pass rates (eval-1 100%, eval-2 100%, eval-3 85%, eval-4 100%, eval-5 100%), overall 91% (54/56), and detailed failing assertions. Line 70: 'Review 40001 (github-actions[bot]) was identified as an eval result review and processed through the eval quality pipeline, not the comment classification pipeline.'

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md classifies comment 50001 from reviewer-b as 'Code Change Request' through normal classification reasoning (directive language, CHANGES_REQUESTED review state, specific implementation guidance). No mention of eval detection or eval metrics in the classification. The Convention Upgrade Evaluation section states 'Not applicable -- the comment is classified directly as a code change request based on the reviewer's language.' report.md line 68 confirms: '| 50001 | reviewer-b | Code change request | Sub-task created (subtask-1) |'

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 Test Quality row includes: 'Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate (11/13); overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56). Failing assertions concern convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation for suggestion-classified review comments.' Lines 50-53 provide detailed assertion text: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility not evaluated for review comment 30002' and (2) 'No sub-task created for review comment 30002 -- classified as suggestion without convention upgrade attempt.'

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md is titled 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation.' Lines 35-41 quote the two failing assertions with their evidence text: Assertion 1 about convention upgrade eligibility (evidence: 'no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented') and Assertion 2 about sub-task creation (evidence: 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002'). report.md line 61 confirms: '| subtask-2 | eval-failure | Fix eval-3 assertion failures: convention upgrade eligibility, sub-task creation |'

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md lines 34-41: '## Review Context' section quotes both assertions verbatim with evidence. Assertion 1: 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)...' with Evidence: 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented in the classification reasoning.' Assertion 2: 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with Evidence: 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted, so the suggestion was not elevated to a code change request.'

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md line 38-39: '## Target PR\nhttps://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. subtask-2.md line 42-43: '## Target PR\nhttps://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. Both action sub-tasks (review-feedback and eval-failure) include the Target PR section pointing to the correct URL.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | 2 root-cause tasks created: convention gap (Markdown documentation convention for CONVENTIONS.md) and skill gap (implement-task convention upgrade pipeline) |'. The verdict is DONE (not N/A). Two root-cause tasks were created: subtask-3 (convention gap) and subtask-4 (skill gap), as confirmed in lines 60-62.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All 14 output files are verification artifacts in /tmp/verify-pr-eval-baseline/eval-6/outputs/: report.md (verification report), review-50001.md (review classification), criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md (acceptance criteria evaluations), subtask-1.md through subtask-4.md (sub-task descriptions). No code files (*.ts, *.js, *.py, *.rs, etc.) or skill definition files (SKILL.md, style-conventions.md) were modified. All outputs are read-only analysis and sub-task description files.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies exactly the 2 files specified in the task: style-conventions.md and SKILL.md' -- compares diff files against task scope, no eval reference. Line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines' -- scans diff for secrets, no eval reference. Line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 7/7 criteria met' -- evaluated in criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md, each analyzing the diff content against specific criteria (new symbol scanning, doc comment verification, verdicts, output format, mapping). None of the 7 criterion files reference eval results or eval metrics.
